Savings Clause, , , provided that:Pub. L. 119–37, div. F, title V, § 6511139 Stat. 648Nov. 12, 2025“Notwithstanding the amendments made by this title [amending this section and sections 355, 355h, 379j–72, and 379j–73 of this title], part 10 of subchapter C of chapter VII of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act ( et seq.), as in effect on the day before the date of enactment of this Act [], shall continue to be in effect with respect to assessing and collecting any fee required by such part for a fiscal year prior to fiscal year 2026.”21 U.S.C. 379j–71Nov. 12, 2025

Finding, , , provided that:Pub. L. 119–37, div. F, title V, § 6502139 Stat. 637Nov. 12, 2025“Congress finds that the fees authorized by the amendments made in this title [amending this section and sections 355, 355h, 379j–72, and 379j–73 of this title] will be dedicated to over-the-counter (OTC) monograph drug activities, as set forth in the goals identified for purposes of part 10 of subchapter C of chapter VII of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act ( et seq.), in the letters from the Secretary of Health and Human Services to the Chairman of the Committee on Energy and Commerce of the House of Representatives and the Chairman of the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions of the Senate, as set forth in the Congressional Record.”21 U.S.C. 379j–71, , , provided that:Pub. children.label: A
children.text: one business entity controls, or has the power to control, the other business entity; or

children.label: B
children.text: a third party controls, or has power to control, both of the business entities.

The term “contract manufacturing organization facility” means an OTC monograph drug facility where neither the owner of such manufacturing facility nor any affiliate of such owner or facility sells the OTC monograph drug produced at such facility directly to wholesalers, retailers, or consumers in the United States.

children.label: A
children.text: officers and employees of the Food and Drug Administration, contractors of the Food and Drug Administration, advisory committees, and costs related to such officers, employees, and committees and costs related to contracts with such contractors;

children.label: B
children.text: management of information, and the acquisition, maintenance, and repair of computer resources;

children.label: C
children.text: leasing, maintenance, renovation, and repair of facilities and acquisition, maintenance, and repair of fixtures, furniture, scientific equipment, and other necessary materials and supplies; and

children.label: D
children.text: collecting fees under and accounting for resources allocated for OTC monograph drug activities.section 379j–72 of this title

The term “FDA establishment identifier” is the unique number automatically generated by Food and Drug Administration’s Field Accomplishments and Compliance Tracking System (FACTS) (or any successor system).
The term “OTC monograph drug” means a nonprescription drug without an approved new drug application which is governed by the provisions of .section 355h of this title

children.label: i
children.text: orders proposing or finalizing applicable conditions of use for OTC monograph drugs;

children.label: ii
children.text: orders affecting status regarding general recognition of safety and effectiveness of an OTC monograph ingredient or combination of ingredients under specified conditions of use;

children.label: iii
children.text: all OTC monograph drug development and review activities, including intra-agency collaboration;

children.label: iv
children.text: regulation and policy development activities related to OTC monograph drugs;

children.label: v
children.text: development of product standards for products subject to review and evaluation;

children.label: vi
children.text: meetings referred to in ;section 355h(i) of this title

children.label: vii
children.text: review of labeling prior to issuance of orders related to OTC monograph drugs or conditions of use; and

children.label: viii
children.text: regulatory science activities related to OTC monograph drugs.

children.label: B
children.text: Inspections related to OTC monograph drugs.

children.label: C
children.text: Monitoring of clinical and other research conducted in connection with OTC monograph drugs.

children.label: i
children.text: collecting, developing, and reviewing safety information on OTC monograph drugs, including adverse event reports;

children.label: ii
children.text: developing and using improved adverse event data-collection systems, including information technology systems; and

children.label: iii
children.text: developing and using improved analytical tools to assess potential safety risks, including access to external databases.

children.label: E
children.text: Other activities necessary for implementation of .section 355h of this title
The term “OTC monograph order request” means a request for an order submitted under .section 355h(b)(5) of this title
The term “Tier 1 OTC monograph order request” means any OTC monograph order request not determined to be a Tier 2 OTC monograph order request.

children.label: i
children.text: the reordering of existing information in the drug facts label of an OTC monograph drug;

children.label: ii
children.text: the addition of information to the other information section of the drug facts label of an OTC monograph drug, as limited by section 201.66(c)(7) of title 21, Code of Federal Regulations (or any successor regulations);

children.label: iii
children.text: modification to the directions for use section of the drug facts label of an OTC monograph drug, if such changes conform to changes made pursuant to ;section 355h(c)(3)(A) of this title

children.label: iv
children.text: the standardization of the concentration or dose of a specific finalized ingredient within a particular finalized monograph;

children.label: v
children.text: a change to ingredient nomenclature to align with nomenclature of a standards-setting organization;

children.label: vi
children.text: the addition of an interchangeable term in accordance with section 330.1 of title 21, Code of Federal Regulations (or any successor regulations); or

children.label: I
children.text: established by a national or international standards development organization; and

children.label: II
children.text: recognized by the Secretary through a process described in guidance for industry, initially published in July 2023, or any successor guidance, publicly available on the website of the Food and Drug Administration, which addresses voluntary consensus standards for pharmaceutical quality.

children.label: B
children.text: The Secretary may, based on program implementation experience or other factors found appropriate by the Secretary, characterize any OTC monograph order request as a Tier 2 OTC monograph order request (including recharacterizing a request from Tier 1 to Tier 2) and publish such determination in a proposed order issued pursuant to .section 355h of this title

children.label: I
children.text: under one management, either direct or indirect; and

children.label: II
children.text: at one geographic location or address engaged in manufacturing or processing the finished dosage form of an OTC monograph drug;

children.label: ii
children.text: includes a finished dosage form manufacturer facility in a contractual relationship with the sponsor of one or more OTC monograph drugs to manufacture or process such drugs; and

children.label: iii
children.text: does not include a business or other entity whose only manufacturing or processing activities are one or more of the following: production of clinical research supplies, testing, or placement of outer packaging on packages containing multiple products, for such purposes as creating multipacks, when each monograph drug product contained within the overpackaging is already in a final packaged form prior to placement in the outer overpackaging.

children.label: i
children.text: closely related to the same business enterprise;

children.label: ii
children.text: under the supervision of the same local management; and

children.label: iii
children.text: under a single FDA establishment identifier and capable of being inspected by the Food and Drug Administration during a single inspection.

children.label: C
children.text: If a business or other entity would meet criteria specified in subparagraph (A), but for being under multiple management, the business or other entity is deemed to constitute multiple facilities, one per management entity, for purposes of this paragraph.
The term “OTC monograph drug meeting” means any meeting regarding the content of a proposed OTC monograph order request.
The term “person” includes an affiliate of a person.
The terms “requestor” and “sponsor” have the meanings given such terms in .section 355h of this title
